model_note: Cursor-subagent invocation; model slug is the one accepted by the Task tool. Closest direct mapping is Anthropic claude-sonnet-4 (i.e., the Sonnet 4 family).
runner_note: 15 Cursor subagents (subagent_type=generalPurpose, model=claude-4-sonnet, one per instance) launched in two parallel batches - 5 numerical, then 10 (5 github_prs + 5 poetry). Each subagent was given the path to a per-instance prompt file under _prompts/ containing the paper Appendix A default template (system + user) wrapped in a roleplay preamble, and instructed to write its literal model output to _responses/<domain>_<id>.txt. Subagent IDs were session-scoped; not archived.

paper.title: AbsenceBench: Language Models Can't Tell What's Missing

validation.shacl_validator_note: tasks/validator.py + tasks/croissant-tasks-shapes.ttl rejects every input across pyshacl 0.22-0.31 due to malformed PropertyShape; see ../../validation_report.json for details.

comparison_to_paper_table_3.note: Paper Table 3 reports micro-F1 across the FULL validation split; ours is on n=5 per subset. Comparison shown for orientation only; the n=5 sample is too small to support claims about model improvement vs. claude-3-7-sonnet.

command_line: cursor: 15 Task() invocations of subagent_type=generalPurpose with model=claude-4-sonnet (see runner_note); subsequently `python _dryrun_ingest.py --model claude-4-sonnet`. Equivalent direct-API command: `python absencebench_implementation.py --dry-run --model claude-sonnet-4-5`.

agent_transcript.note: Lives on the contributor's machine; session-scoped Cursor metadata; not portable. Referenced for audit but not part of this commit. The implementation script + this manifest + the per-instance outputs_*.jsonl are sufficient for full reproducibility.
